title: lepia glastifolia
intro: Lepia glastifolia, also known as Leptandra or American cowslip, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. Its roots and rhizomes possess medicinal properties, containing iridoid glycosides and sesquiterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Lepia glastifolia is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory problems, and supporting the digestive system. In herbal preparations, the roots are typically dried and used in infusions, tinctures, or teas to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
